title: China Advances Pure IPv6 Network and Surveillance-Enabled IPv6+
summary: On July 21, 2026, China&\#x27;s Cyberspace Administration released a plan to achieve 900 million active IPv6 users by 2027 and transition to a pure IPv6 single-stack network by 2030, while advancing IPv6+ which embeds metadata for surveillance. This policy could reshape global internet governance by normalizing state-controlled network architectures, and raises significant privacy concerns as IPv6+ enables content metadata inspection and routing manipulation. IPv6+ allows embedding content metadata and suggested route paths in packets, which experts say enables censorship, precision blocking, or additional billing. China&\#x27;s equipment vendors have already exported IPv6+-capable devices to multiple countries.
context: IPv6, the latest Internet Protocol version, provides vastly more addresses than IPv4. China has long pursued its own internet standards, including the earlier &\#x27;New IP&\#x27; proposal at the ITU. A pure IPv6 single-stack network eliminates reliance on IPv4, simplifying network management but enabling central control.
